main_turf.bias_notes: Counter-Clockwise. An outstanding, expansive provincial track. It features wide, sweeping turns and a generous home straight. It is universally regarded as one of the fairest tracks in Australia, giving every horse (leaders and deep closers) a legitimate chance.
main_turf.post_bias: Incredibly fair overall. The only slight disadvantage is for horses drawn very wide (10+) in the 1100m and 1200m sprint chutes, as they can occasionally get caught 3-wide without cover on the sweeping turn.

handicapping_angles.the_fair_track_factor: Because Sale is so spacious, one-dimensional front-runners who rely on tight turns to steal races are highly vulnerable here. Heavily upgrade 'Swoopers' (closers) and horses with long, sustained galloping strides.
handicapping_angles.the_metro_raid: Elite Melbourne metropolitan trainers (from Caulfield, Cranbourne, and Pakenham) constantly send their promising horses to Sale for 'easy' provincial kills. Always respect horses dropping from Metro midweek grades into Sale handicaps.
handicapping_angles.winter_heavy_form: During the Victorian winter, Sale can become incredibly deep and heavy. When the track is rated Heavy 8 or worse, look for proven 'mudlarks' and downgrade horses whose only wins are on Good (dry) surfaces.
